notes: Expanded BMW DTC dictionary curated for E65 N52B30 (GCC spec) plus common N52/N54/N55/B58 codes. severity: low | medium | high | critical. diy: 1 (easy DIY) to 5 (specialist). BMW-specific codes (P1xxx, P2xxx, BMW UDS) follow OBD generic.

P0010.title: VANOS intake actuator (Bank 1)
P0010.cause: VANOS solenoid clogged or seal failure. Common on N52 over 100k km. Clean solenoid with brake cleaner first, replace if no improvement.

P0011.title: Intake cam timing over-advanced
P0011.cause: VANOS unit worn or oil pressure low. Check oil level first, then solenoid.

P0012.title: Intake cam timing over-retarded
P0012.cause: VANOS solenoid sticking, sludge in oil galleries. Italian tune-up + oil change sometimes helps.

P0013.title: Exhaust VANOS solenoid (Bank 1)
P0013.cause: Exhaust VANOS solenoid electrical fault. Check wiring connector first.

P0014.title: Exhaust cam timing over-advanced
P0014.cause: Exhaust VANOS solenoid stuck — clean or replace. Same family as P0011.

P0015.title: Exhaust cam timing over-retarded
P0015.cause: Exhaust VANOS solenoid sticking due to dirty oil. Change oil + filter, may clear itself.

P0016.title: Crank/cam correlation (Bank 1, Sensor A)
P0016.cause: Stretched timing chain (classic N52/N51 fault), worn guides, or cam sensor failure. Listen for rattle on cold start.

P0017.title: Crank/cam correlation (Bank 1, Sensor B)
P0017.cause: Timing chain stretch on exhaust side. Plan repair before chain skips a tooth — that means new engine.

P0020.title: VANOS actuator circuit B (Bank 1)
P0020.cause: Exhaust VANOS solenoid open or short circuit.

P0021.title: Bank 1 camshaft B over-advanced
P0021.cause: Exhaust cam over-advanced — VANOS oil control valve.

P0022.title: Bank 1 camshaft B over-retarded
P0022.cause: Exhaust cam over-retarded — VANOS oil control valve.

P0030.title: O2 heater control (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
P0030.cause: Pre-cat O2 sensor heater circuit. Common after 100k km.

P0031.title: O2 heater low (B1 S1)
P0031.cause: O2 sensor heater shorted to ground.

P0032.title: O2 heater high (B1 S1)
P0032.cause: O2 sensor heater shorted to power.

P0036.title: O2 heater control (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
P0036.cause: Post-cat O2 sensor heater. Doesn't usually cause running issues.

P0037.title: O2 heater low (B1 S2)
P0037.cause: Post-cat O2 sensor heater shorted to ground.

P0038.title: O2 heater high (B1 S2)
P0038.cause: Post-cat O2 sensor heater shorted to power.

P0100.title: MAF sensor circuit
P0100.cause: Hot-film MAF dirty or disconnected. Try cleaning with MAF cleaner (€10) before replacing (€150).

P0101.title: MAF range/performance
P0101.cause: MAF reading implausible — dirty sensor, intake leak (CCV/DISA on N52), or cracked intake boot.

P0102.title: MAF circuit low input
P0102.cause: MAF sensor disconnected or wiring break.

P0107.title: MAP sensor low
P0107.cause: MAP sensor or vacuum line — N52 mainly relies on MAF, but MAP is present.

P0108.title: MAP sensor high
P0108.cause: MAP sensor wiring shorted to power.

P0111.title: IAT sensor range/performance
P0111.cause: Intake air temp reading drifting. Often combined with MAF on N52.

P0113.title: IAT sensor high input
P0113.cause: IAT sensor open-circuit. Often integrated in MAF on N52 — replace MAF unit.

P0115.title: Engine coolant temp circuit
P0115.cause: Coolant temp sensor wiring or sensor itself.

P0116.title: Coolant temp performance
P0116.cause: Reading drift. Combined with stuck thermostat usually.

P0117.title: Coolant temp sensor low
P0117.cause: Sensor or wiring short. May cause cold-start enrichment failure.

P0118.title: Coolant temp sensor high
P0118.cause: Sensor open or wiring break. Engine may run in limp mode.

P0120.title: Throttle position sensor circuit
P0120.cause: Throttle body (DK-Motor) potentiometer fault. Common on N52 above 150k km — full throttle body replacement €400-600.

P0121.title: Throttle range/performance
P0121.cause: TPS reading inconsistent with pedal demand. Limp mode likely.

P0128.title: Coolant below regulating temp (thermostat)
P0128.cause: Stuck-open thermostat. Very common on N52 above 120k km. Engine runs cold = poor MPG. Replace thermostat €100 + 2h labour.

P0130.title: O2 sensor (Bank 1 Sensor 1) circuit
P0130.cause: Pre-cat O2 sensor failing. Replace sensor €100. Bosch OEM only.

P0133.title: O2 sensor slow response
P0133.cause: Aged pre-cat O2 sensor. Affects fuel trim accuracy. Replace, don't try to clean.

P0134.title: O2 sensor no activity (B1 S1)
P0134.cause: O2 sensor stuck mid-range. Heater failure or sensor dead.

P0135.title: O2 heater circuit (B1 S1)
P0135.cause: Heater element open. Sensor still reads after warm-up but readiness won't complete.

P0136.title: O2 sensor (B1 S2) circuit
P0136.cause: Post-cat O2 sensor — monitor only, not used for fuel trim on N52.

P0141.title: O2 heater circuit (B1 S2)
P0141.cause: Post-cat O2 heater failure. Replace sensor.

P0171.title: System too lean (Bank 1)
P0171.cause: Vacuum leak — CCV/oil separator (very common on N52), DISA gasket, intake boot crack, dirty MAF, or weak fuel pump.

P0172.title: System too rich (Bank 1)
P0172.cause: Leaky injector, high fuel pressure, or MAF underreading. Less common than lean.

P0174.title: System too lean (Bank 2)
P0174.cause: N52 is inline-6 so only Bank 1 exists. P0174 indicates wrong protocol identification.

P0201.title: Injector 1 circuit open
P0201.cause: Injector electrical fault or wiring break. Risk of cylinder washout — fix promptly.

P0218.title: Transmission over-temp
P0218.cause: GA6HP19Z gearbox overheating — check ATF level (sealed system, needs specialist), cooler block, or torque converter slip.

P0234.title: Turbo overboost
P0234.cause: N54/N55 only. N52 is naturally aspirated — should not see this code.

P0299.title: Turbo underboost
P0299.cause: N54/N55 only — wastegate, diverter valve, charge pipe leak.

P0300.title: Random multi-cylinder misfire
P0300.cause: Plugs/coils worn (N52 every 60-80k km), vacuum leak, or fuel pressure low. Start with plugs €60.

P0301.title: Cylinder 1 misfire
P0301.cause: Coil or plug on cyl 1. Swap coil with cyl 2 — if misfire follows, it's the coil.

P0302.title: Cylinder 2 misfire
P0302.cause: Coil or plug on cyl 2. Same swap-test approach.

P0313.title: Misfire low fuel
P0313.cause: Fuel level too low for accurate misfire detection. Fill up.

P0315.title: Crank position variation not learned
P0315.cause: Drive 5 min steady highway after battery disconnect — DME relearns.

P0325.title: Knock sensor 1 circuit (Bank 1)
P0325.cause: Knock sensor or wiring. Engine pulls timing as safety measure — power loss.

P0326.title: Knock sensor 1 range
P0326.cause: Knock sensor signal noisy — loose mounting bolt is classic cause on N52.

P0327.title: Knock sensor 1 low
P0327.cause: Knock sensor wiring short to ground.

P0330.title: Knock sensor 2 circuit
P0330.cause: Second knock sensor — N52 has two.

P0335.title: Crankshaft position sensor circuit
P0335.cause: Crank sensor or wiring. Can cause no-start or stall. Sensor €60.

P0336.title: Crank sensor range
P0336.cause: Crank sensor signal erratic — possible reluctor wheel damage.

P0340.title: Camshaft position sensor
P0340.cause: Cam sensor fault. Often paired with stretched timing chain symptoms.

P0341.title: Cam sensor performance
P0341.cause: Cam signal out of expected range.

P0345.title: Cam sensor Bank 2
P0345.cause: N52 inline-6 has Bank 1 only. Wiring confusion likely.

P0410.title: Secondary air injection system
P0410.cause: Secondary air pump (cold-start emissions) — common failure, but car runs fine. €300 pump + valve.

P0411.title: Secondary air flow incorrect
P0411.cause: Same family — air pump weak, check valves stuck, or hoses cracked.

P0420.title: Catalyst efficiency below threshold (Bank 1)
P0420.cause: Aged cat OR aged post-cat O2 sensor. ALWAYS replace post-cat O2 sensor first (€100) before condemning cat (€800-1500).

P0440.title: EVAP system malfunction
P0440.cause: Loose fuel cap is the classic cause. Tighten and clear.

P0441.title: EVAP incorrect purge flow
P0441.cause: Purge valve stuck open/closed. Replace valve.

P0442.title: EVAP small leak
P0442.cause: Fuel cap, vapor line, or LDP (leak detection pump) fault. Smoke test the EVAP system.

P0444.title: EVAP purge valve open
P0444.cause: Stuck-open purge valve — vacuum leak symptoms.

P0455.title: EVAP large leak
P0455.cause: Fuel cap not seated, cracked vapor line, or pump.

P0456.title: EVAP very small leak
P0456.cause: Pinhole leak somewhere in EVAP system. Smoke test needed.

P0500.title: Vehicle speed sensor
P0500.cause: VSS or wheel-speed sensor. Speedometer dropouts — check DSC module DTCs for individual wheel codes.

P0501.title: VSS range/performance
P0501.cause: Speed signal inconsistent. Multiple wheel sensors likely.

P0506.title: Idle lower than expected
P0506.cause: Vacuum leak or dirty throttle body. Clean DISA / throttle plate.

P0507.title: Idle higher than expected
P0507.cause: Vacuum leak, throttle plate stuck open, or DISA flap broken.

P0601.title: Internal ECM memory checksum
P0601.cause: DME corruption. Try battery disconnect to reset; if persistent, ECU may be failing.

P0606.title: ECM processor fault
P0606.cause: DME internal failure. ECU replacement + coding by specialist likely needed €€€.

P0700.title: Transmission control system
P0700.cause: EGS module reports a fault. Use Diagnose → DSC/EGS scan to read transmission-specific codes.

P0730.title: Incorrect gear ratio
P0730.cause: Trans slipping. ATF condition + level check needed (specialist).

P0741.title: Torque converter clutch stuck off
P0741.cause: Lockup clutch fault. ATF service may help; otherwise rebuild.

P1083.title: Fuel trim lean stop (Bank 1)
P1083.cause: BMW-specific lean condition at idle. Often CCV/oil separator on N52 (€80 part, 4h labour).

P1085.title: Fuel trim rich stop (Bank 1)
P1085.cause: BMW-specific rich condition. Check injectors, fuel pressure.

P1101.title: MAF sensor plausibility (BMW)
P1101.cause: BMW-specific — MAF reading vs throttle position mismatch.

P1183.title: Mixture adaptation Bank 1 (BMW)
P1183.cause: BMW adaptation has hit max correction. Lean condition root cause needed.

P1188.title: Mixture control 1 / adaptation
P1188.cause: Long-term fuel trim out of range.

P1397.title: Camshaft sensor signal exhaust (BMW)
P1397.cause: Exhaust cam sensor or VANOS position.

P1496.title: Heated O2 sensor heater Bank 1 Sensor 2

P1520.title: Camshaft position actuator (BMW)
P1520.cause: VANOS solenoid stuck on intake side.

P1542.title: Throttle pedal sensor implausible
P1542.cause: BMW-specific accelerator pedal sensor fault. Two redundant sensors disagree.

P1564.title: Throttle valve adaptation lower limit
P1564.cause: Throttle body needs adaptation reset — clean + INPA reset.

P2096.title: Post-cat fuel trim lean (Bank 1)
P2096.cause: Mild exhaust leak before post-cat O2 or aging cat.

P2097.title: Post-cat fuel trim rich (Bank 1)
P2097.cause: Post-cat O2 sees rich condition — aging cat usually.

P2177.title: System too lean off idle Bank 1
P2177.cause: Lean condition under load — vacuum leak or fuel delivery weak.

P2187.title: System too lean at idle Bank 1
P2187.cause: Idle vacuum leak — CCV/oil separator very common on N52.

P2188.title: System too rich at idle Bank 1
P2188.cause: Idle rich — leaky injector or fuel pressure too high.

P2189.title: System too lean at idle Bank 2
P2189.cause: Bank 2 reading on inline-6 — protocol mismatch.

P3081.title: Engine temp too low for combustion
P3081.cause: Stuck-open thermostat — same as P0128. Replace thermostat.

U0001.title: High speed CAN communication bus
U0001.cause: D-CAN bus issue. Module-to-module communication failure.

U0100.title: Lost communication with ECM/PCM (DME)
U0100.cause: DME offline. Check power, ground, CAN wiring.

U0140.title: Lost communication with body control module

U0155.title: Lost communication with instrument cluster
U0155.cause: KOMBI cluster offline — K-CAN issue likely.

_BMW_E65_NOTES._: Codes starting with non-standard hex like 2F4100, 5E20XX etc are BMW UDS DTCs from non-DME modules. Use the BMW module DTC scan (Diagnose → Full Scan) to see DSC/EGS-specific codes. The OBD generic dictionary above covers DME (engine) faults only.

2A82.title: VANOS intake reaction time (BMW UDS)
2A82.cause: BMW UDS code — VANOS intake slow response. Same family as P0010/P0011.

2C9F.title: Crankcase ventilation oil separator (BMW)
2C9F.cause: BMW UDS — CCV/oil separator faulty (very common on N52). Causes P0171/P2187 too.

2D06.title: DISA valve electrical (BMW UDS)
2D06.cause: DISA (intake runner) actuator electrical fault. €150 part.

2D24.title: Oxygen sensor before cat aging (BMW)
2D24.cause: BMW UDS — pre-cat O2 sensor aged. Replace €100.

29CD.title: Misfire from cyl 1 (BMW UDS)
29CD.cause: BMW UDS extended cylinder 1 misfire — same family as P0301.

29F2.title: Knock control Bank 1 (BMW)
29F2.cause: BMW UDS knock control — bad fuel or knock sensor.

27A0.title: Air mass meter signal (BMW UDS)
27A0.cause: BMW UDS — MAF sensor reading implausible. Clean or replace.

5DF0.title: DSC wheel speed sensor front-left
5DF0.cause: DSC module reports front-left wheel speed sensor fault. Replace sensor €60.

5E20.title: DSC longitudinal acceleration sensor
5E20.cause: DSC module — longitudinal G sensor. Internal to DSC unit.

5E21.title: DSC yaw rate sensor
5E21.cause: DSC — yaw sensor. Internal to DSC unit on E65.

5E40.title: DSC pressure sensor
5E40.cause: Brake pressure sensor in master cylinder.

4F85.title: EGS solenoid valve 1 (BMW)
4F85.cause: ZF 6HP gearbox solenoid — mechatronic unit. €1000+ specialist job.

4F94.title: EGS torque converter clutch
4F94.cause: TCC solenoid — symptoms include shuddering at lockup.
